## Overview

Spreading activation is the mechanism by which thinking about one concept makes related concepts more accessible. When you retrieve "doctor" from memory, "nurse" and "hospital" become slightly more available — that's spreading activation at work.

The model originated with Collins and Loftus and underpins retrieval dynamics in cognitive architectures like [[ACT-R]].
